subject: Chemistry
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
(A) is correct but (R) is not correct.

solution:
Gypsum board is also known as plasterboard, drywall or wall board. It consists of the paper surface and non-combustible core. These boards are easy to install. It has excellent fire resistance. Helps in sound isolation by preventing the transfer of unnecessary sound. It is less expensive and has great durability.

When exposed to high temperatures, gypsum only delays the temperature rise and cannot prevent shrinkage and crack from occurring, which can lead to the eventual collapse or failure of gypsum-based products.

Hence, both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).

subject: Chemistry
solution: As we down in a group then its size increases and also volume of the atom increases as the number of electrons and protons increases. But the increase in mass is greater than that of increase in volume so the density which is defined as mass per unit volume will increase generally. Density of alkali metals increases down the group except potassium. Therefore, correct order of density of given alkali metals is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mo><</mo><mi>Na</mi><mo><</mo><mi>Rb</mi><mo><</mo><mi>Cs</mi></math>

subject: Chemistry
options: To facilitate the hydration of cement
To slow down the process of setting
To speed up the process of setting
solution: When mixed with water the setting of cement takes place to give a hard mass. The is due to the hydration of molecules of the constituents and their rearrangement. Gypsum is CaSO4.2H2O. The purpose of adding gypsum is only to slow down the process of setting of the cement so that it gets sufficiently hardened.

subject: Chemistry
options: Concentration of $\mathrm{CO}_3{ }^{2-}$ ions is very low.
Sodium ions will react with acid radicals.
$\mathrm{Na}^{+}$ions will interfere with the detection of $\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}, \mathrm{Ba}^{2+}, \mathrm{Sr}^{2+}$ ions.
solution: If $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ is used in place of $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$. It will precipitate group $\mathrm{V}$ radicals as well as magnesium radicals. The reason for this is the high ionization of $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ in water into $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$and $\mathrm{CO}_3^{--}$. Now the higher concentration of $\mathrm{CO}_3^{--}$is available which exceeds the solubility product of group $\mathrm{V}$ radicals as well as that of magnesium radicals.

solution: Cement or Portland cement mainly contains oxides of calcium, silicon, aluminium, and iron. When water is mixed with cement, the oxides get hydrated, and cement sets extremely fast. To control the setting time of cement, gypsum is added to it, which increases the setting time of cement.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Hydration enthalpy is inversely related to the size of the ion.

Li+ has maximum hydration enthalpy in group 1 due to its small size.

Lithium shows diagonal relationship to magnesium.

The similarity between lithium and magnesium is particularly striking and arises because of their similar sizes : atomic radii, Li = 152 pm, Mg = 160 pm; ionic radii : Li+ = 76 pm, Mg2+= 72 pm.

The main points of similarity are

(I)Both lithium and magnesium are harder and lighter (ii) Lithium and magnesium react slowly with water. Their oxides and hydroxides are much less soluble and their hydroxides decompose on heating. Both form a nitride, Li3N and Mg3N2 , by direct combination with nitrogen. (iii) The oxides, Li2O and MgO do not combine with excess oxygen to give any superoxide. (iv) The carbonates of lithium and magnesium decompose easily on heating to form the oxides and CO2 .

Solid hydrogencarbonates are not formed by lithium and magnesium. (v) Both LiCl and MgCl2 are soluble in ethanol. (vi) Both LiCl and MgCl2 are deliquescent and crystallise from aqueous solution as hydrates, LiCl·2H2O and MgCl2 ·6H2O.

So 'B' is Mg.

B. Beryllium carbonate is kept in the atmosphere of CO2 because it is thermally less stable.

BeCO3 ⇌ BeO + CO2

C. Beryllium sulphate is readily soluble in water due to high degree of hydration.

D. Beryllium shows anomalous behaviour due to small size, high ionization energy and high value of ϕ (polarising power).

So, the correct statement is B, C and D only.

K+ / K → -2.93V

Rb+ / Rb → -2.98V

Na+ / Na → -2.71V

Li+ / Li → -3.05V

Among the given alkali metals, sodium metal is the weakest reducing agent as its standard reduction potential is least negative which is ENa+/Na° = -2.71

So, the correct option is C.

BeOH2+SrOH2⟶Sr+2Be(OH)4-2

As Be is present in anionic part.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The hydration enthalpy of alkali metal ions decreases with an increase in ionic sizes.

So, the order is: Li+ > Na+ >  K+ > Rb+ > Cs+

Like alkali metal ions, the hydration enthalpies of alkaline earth metal ions decrease with an increase in ionic size down the group.

So, the order is: Be2+ > Mg2+ > Ca2+ > Sr2+ > Ba2+ 

The hydration enthalpies of alkaline earth metal ions are larger than those of alkali metal ions. 

So, the correct order is: Mg2+ > Ca2+ > K+ > Rb+ > Cs+

subject: Chemistry
solution:
All the elements except beryllium combine with hydrogen upon heating to form their hydrides, MH2. BeH2, however, can be prepared by the reaction of BeCl2 with LiAlH4. .

2BeCl2+LiAlH4→2BeH2+LiCl+AlCl3
